shared_properties: Gate footprint stays 32x32px to match the escape object size in the GDD.
Dungeon palette remains dark around the edges so the goal aperture can glow clearly.
Outline weight stays crisp at 1px for stable read at 4x upscale.
differences: Sprite sheet is 192x32px with six pulse frames in a single row.
Gold gate bars and interior light ramp up and down to signal the final objective.
A bright inner aperture contrasts with the surrounding abyss tones to pull attention downward.

context.usage: Goal object placed in the deepest shaft segment to trigger the clear state on contact.

shape.silhouette: Square barred gate with a bright inner opening and stone border.
shape.collision_read: Outer frame keeps a stable 32x32 silhouette while the interior pulse changes.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet size is exactly 192x32px with six 32x32px frames.
The inner light pulse stays readable at 6 FPS without obscuring the gate bars.
The goal object remains visibly distinct from hazard colors through gold-dominant highlights.

reference_images.label: slip_down_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds stone texture breakup, low-key lighting, and vertical shaft composition for the active environment asset.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteSheetBundle on a goal entity with a separate trigger collider.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load through AssetServer during the Loading state and bind it to the exit gate prefab.
runtime_notes: Use Bevy 0.13 with ImagePlugin::default_nearest() so every asset stays pixel-perfect at 4x upscale.
Drive gameplay motion in FixedUpdate at 60 FPS and keep art timing on deterministic timers.
Keep clear-trigger logic in gameplay code and let the sprite sheet communicate discoverability only.
